edges.description: Feedback-resistant PRS-I increases PRPP availability and de novo purine nucleotide synthesis.
Severe PRS-I superactivity with allosteric variants can include neurodevelopmental and auditory findings through incompletely defined downstream mechanisms.
PRS1 overexpression increases PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis in intact patient cells.
Increased purine nucleotide flux increases downstream uric acid production.
Serum urate rises because uric acid production exceeds clearance.
Urinary uric acid excretion rises with urate overproduction.
Sustained hyperuricemia and hyperuricosuria allow uric acid to crystallize when serum urate is not controlled.
Persistent hyperuricemia promotes urate crystal arthritis.
Hyperuricosuria and uric acid crystalluria promote urinary stones.
Urate overproduction can first manifest as urinary uric acid crystals.
Severe urate crystal disease can cause acute kidney failure.
Severe PRS-I superactivity can include developmental delay or intellectual disability.
Severe PRS-I superactivity can include sensorineural hearing loss.
Severe PRS-I superactivity can include hypotonia.
Severe PRS-I superactivity can include ataxia.
Increased PRS-I activity or abnormal allosteric regulation reports the proximal PRS-I superactivity mechanism.
edges.target: Increased PRS-I activity or impaired allosteric regulation

edges.description: Increased PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis directly report the purine-overproduction node downstream of PRS-I superactivity.
edges.target: Increased PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis

edges.description: Elevated serum urate reports the urate-overproduction branch and is used to monitor crystal-disease risk.
Increased urinary uric acid excretion reports urate overproduction and the urinary crystal/stone-risk branch.

edges.source: PRPS1 transcriptional overactivity with structurally normal PRS1

nodes.description: Severe urate overproduction with nephrolithiasis can precipitate acute kidney failure.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: We describe a 16-year-old African American female who developed progressive tophi, nephrolithiasis and acute kidney failure due to urate overproduction.

nodes.description: Ataxia occurs in the severe phenotype as part of neurologic involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: whereas the severe phenotype (~25% of affected males) with onset in the first decade of life has in addition to these biochemical findings variable combinations of developmental delay (DD) / intellectual disability (ID), sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: Clinical findings in previously reported females with PRPS1 superactivity showed a high clinical penetrance of this disorder with a mean serum urate level of 8.5 (4.1) mg/dl

readouts.interpretation: Elevated serum urate reports the urate-overproduction branch and is used to monitor crystal-disease risk.

nodes.description: Gouty arthritis develops when serum urate is not adequately controlled, and can be severe or tophaceous in affected females and males.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: In the mild phenotype, uric acid crystalluria or a urinary stone is commonly the first clinical finding, followed later by gouty arthritis if serum urate concentration is not controlled.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: Family history included a mother with tophaceous gout who developed end-stage kidney disease due to nephrolithiasis and an affected sister with polyarticular gout.

nodes.description: Elevated serum urate from PRS-I-driven uric acid overproduction. This is a defining biochemical feature in both mild and severe PRS-I superactivity.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase (PRS) superactivity comprises two phenotypes, both characterized by hyperuricemia and hyperuricosuria.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: Clinical findings in previously reported females with PRPS1 superactivity showed a high clinical penetrance of this disorder with a mean serum urate level of 8.5 (4.1) mg/dl

nodes.description: Increased urinary uric acid excretion accompanies hyperuricemia and contributes to uric acid crystalluria and urinary stones.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase (PRS) superactivity comprises two phenotypes, both characterized by hyperuricemia and hyperuricosuria.

nodes.description: Hypotonia occurs in the severe phenotype as part of neurologic involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: whereas the severe phenotype (~25% of affected males) with onset in the first decade of life has in addition to these biochemical findings variable combinations of developmental delay (DD) / intellectual disability (ID), sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia.

nodes.id: Increased PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: modulated relative increases in PRS activities at suboptimal Pi concentration and in rates of PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis in intact patient fibroblasts indicate that despite an intact allosteric mechanism of regulation of PRS activity, PRPS1 transcription is a major determinant of PRPP and purine synthesis.

readouts.interpretation: Increased PRPP and purine nucleotide synthesis directly report the purine-overproduction node downstream of PRS-I superactivity.

nodes.id: Increased PRS-I activity or impaired allosteric regulation

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: detection of high activity or lack of allosteric regulation of the PRS-I enzyme (PRS-I enzyme assay) establishes the diagnosis.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic and functional basis of purine nucleotide feedback-resistant phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Kinetic analysis of recombinant mutant PRS1s showed that widely dispersed point mutations in the X chromosome-linked PRPS1 gene encoding the PRS1 isoform result in alteration of the allosteric mechanisms regulating both enzyme inhibition by purine nucleotides and activation by inorganic phosphate.

readouts.interpretation: Increased PRS-I activity or abnormal allosteric regulation reports the proximal PRS-I superactivity mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: In male relatives at risk: measurement of serum urate concentration and 24-hour urinary uric acid excretion or spot urinary urate-to-creatinine ratio.

readouts.interpretation: Increased urinary uric acid excretion reports urate overproduction and the urinary crystal/stone-risk branch.

nodes.description: Severe PRS-I superactivity can include developmental delay or intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: whereas the severe phenotype (~25% of affected males) with onset in the first decade of life has in addition to these biochemical findings variable combinations of developmental delay (DD) / intellectual disability (ID), sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia.

nodes.description: Severe PRS-I superactivity can involve the nervous system and hearing, including developmental delay or intellectual disability, sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia. The connecting mechanism from purine dysregulation to these features is not fully defined.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: whereas the severe phenotype (~25% of affected males) with onset in the first decade of life has in addition to these biochemical findings variable combinations of developmental delay (DD) / intellectual disability (ID), sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: Association of PRPS1 Mutations with Disease Phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: The overexpression of PRPS1 results in PRS-I superactivity resulting in purine overproduction. Patients with PRS-I superactivity demonstrate uric acid overproduction, hypotonia, ataxia, neurodevelopment abnormalities, and postlingual hearing impairment.

nodes.description: Excess PRS-I activity increases PRPP availability and drives de novo purine nucleotide biosynthesis beyond metabolic demand, creating the biochemical substrate for uric acid overproduction.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic and functional basis of purine nucleotide feedback-resistant phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity.
evidence.snippet: The functional consequences of these mutations provide a tenable basis for the enhanced production of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate, purine nucleotides, and uric acid that are the biochemical hallmarks of PRS superactivity.

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate (PRPP) synthetase (PRS) superactivity is an X-linked disorder characterized by gout with overproduction of purine nucleotides and uric acid.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic and functional basis of purine nucleotide feedback-resistant phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity.
evidence.snippet: each PRS1 cDNA contained a distinctive single base substitution predicting a corresponding amino acid substitution in the PRS1 isoform.

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: Study of the two X-linked PRS isoforms (PRS1 and PRS2) in cells from certain affected individuals has shown selectively increased concentrations of structurally normal PRS1 transcript and isoform, suggesting that this form of the disorder involves pretranslational dysregulation of PRPS1 expression and might be more appropriately termed overactivity of normal PRS.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: PRS superactivity caused by a pathogenic variant in PRPS1 is inherited in an X-linked manner.

evidence.reference_title: PRPS1 / phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity (Limited)
evidence.snippet: PRPS1 | HGNC:9462 | phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity | MONDO:0010395 | XL | Limited

nodes.description: Missense variants alter PRS-I allosteric regulation, including reduced inhibition by purine nucleotides such as ADP/GDP.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: Mutational analysis revealed a new c.520 G > A (p.G174R) mutation in the PRPS1 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: The mutation resulted in decreased PRPS1 inhibition by ADP.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic and functional basis of purine nucleotide feedback-resistant phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Kinetic analysis of recombinant mutant PRS1s showed that widely dispersed point mutations in the X chromosome-linked PRPS1 gene encoding the PRS1 isoform result in alteration of the allosteric mechanisms regulating both enzyme inhibition by purine nucleotides and activation by inorganic phosphate.

nodes.description: In the mild overactivity-of-normal-PRS mechanism, patient cells have increased PRPS1 transcription and PRS1 isoform expression despite normal PRS-I allosteric regulation, increasing PRPP and purine synthesis.

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: PRPS1 transcription was increased relative to GAPDH (3- to 4-fold normal in fibroblasts; 1.9- to 2.4-fold in lymphoblasts) and PRPS2.

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: accelerated PRPS1 transcription is the major aberration leading to PRS1 overexpression.

nodes.description: Some affected males have increased PRPS1 transcription and PRS1 overexpression without a detectable PRPS1 coding variant.
nodes.id: PRPS1 transcriptional overactivity with structurally normal PRS1

evidence.reference_title: Accelerated transcription of PRPS1 in X-linked overactivity of normal human phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase.
evidence.snippet: Neither PRPS1 amplification nor altered stability or processing of PRS1 mRNA was identified, but PRPS1 transcription was increased relative to GAPDH (3- to 4-fold normal in fibroblasts; 1.9- to 2.4-fold in lymphoblasts) and PRPS2.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: PRS superactivity caused by elevated PRPS1 mRNA levels is also inherited in an X-linked manner; however, because the underlying genetic alteration has not been characterized, the mode of inheritance cannot be confirmed with certainty.

nodes.description: PRPS1 missense variants can make PRS-I resistant to normal purine nucleotide feedback inhibition and alter activation by inorganic phosphate. This allosteric gain of function increases effective PRS-I activity in patient cells and recombinant PRS1 models.

evidence.reference_title: The genetic and functional basis of purine nucleotide feedback-resistant phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Kinetic analysis of recombinant mutant PRS1s showed that widely dispersed point mutations in the X chromosome-linked PRPS1 gene encoding the PRS1 isoform result in alteration of the allosteric mechanisms regulating both enzyme inhibition by purine nucleotides and activation by inorganic phosphate.

evidence.reference_title: Human X-linked phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity is associated with distinct point mutations in the PRPS1 gene.
evidence.snippet: recombinant N. B. and S. M. PRS1s showed the purine nucleotide feedback resistance phenotypes characteristic of PRS from patients' cells.

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing loss is a severe-phenotype manifestation and may be postlingual in PRS-I superactivity.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: whereas the severe phenotype (~25% of affected males) with onset in the first decade of life has in addition to these biochemical findings variable combinations of developmental delay (DD) / intellectual disability (ID), sensorineural hearing loss, hypotonia, and ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: Association of PRPS1 Mutations with Disease Phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: The overexpression of PRPS1 results in PRS-I superactivity resulting in purine overproduction. Patients with PRS-I superactivity demonstrate uric acid overproduction, hypotonia, ataxia, neurodevelopment abnormalities, and postlingual hearing impairment.

nodes.description: Excess purine turnover increases uric acid production, causing hyperuricemia and hyperuricosuria. This is the biochemical state that precedes, but is distinct from, uric acid crystal deposition.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase (PRS) superactivity comprises two phenotypes, both characterized by hyperuricemia and hyperuricosuria.

nodes.description: When urate overproduction is not controlled, uric acid crystallizes in urine, kidneys, and joints, driving crystalluria, stones, gouty arthritis, tophi, and crystal-related kidney injury.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical manifestations and molecular aspects of phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase superactivity in females.
evidence.snippet: We describe a 16-year-old African American female who developed progressive tophi, nephrolithiasis and acute kidney failure due to urate overproduction.

nodes.description: Uric acid crystalluria can be the first clinical finding in the mild phenotype, reflecting urinary uric acid supersaturation before overt gout.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: In the mild phenotype, uric acid crystalluria or a urinary stone is commonly the first clinical finding, followed later by gouty arthritis if serum urate concentration is not controlled.

nodes.description: Uric acid urinary stones and nephrolithiasis arise from increased urinary uric acid excretion and crystalluria.

evidence.reference_title: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate Synthetase Superactivity.
evidence.snippet: In the mild phenotype, uric acid crystalluria or a urinary stone is commonly the first clinical finding, followed later by gouty arthritis if serum urate concentration is not controlled.

evidence.snippet: We describe a 16-year-old African American female who developed progressive tophi, nephrolithiasis and acute kidney failure due to urate overproduction.
